title: Blue Man Group
blurb: The wordless, paint-drumming, comedic spectacle on International Drive. Runs most nights.

title: Cirque du Soleil: Drawn to Life
blurb: Cirque + Disney animation: acrobatics built around a hand-drawn story. Resident show at Disney Springs.

title: Baldwin Park Independence Bash
blurb: Your own neighborhood's July 4th bash — music, food and fireworks right at the Village Center. Walkable.

title: America 250 High Tea Cruise (Barbara Lee)
location: St. Johns Rivership Co., 433 N Palmetto Ave
blurb: High tea aboard a riverboat cruising the St. Johns River out of historic Sanford.

title: Star Spangled Sanford
blurb: July 4th fireworks over Lake Monroe with food vendors and live music on the Sanford riverfront.

title: Firework Dinner Cruise (Barbara Lee)
blurb: Dinner cruise on the St. Johns with fireworks views from the water.

title: Mango's 4th of July Block Party
blurb: All-day Independence Day block party with DJs, dancers and drinks on International Drive.

title: Orlando Cars & Coffee — Henry's Depot
blurb: Morning car meet at Henry's Depot in historic downtown Sanford — exotics, classics, coffee.

title: Forever My R&B Block Party (All White)
blurb: All-white-attire R&B block party in downtown Orlando's Wall Street Plaza.

title: Thornton Park 2nd Thursday Wine & Art Walk
blurb: Wine pours, art vendors, live music, night market — a quick hop from Baldwin Park. 21+ to sample.

title: The Caymus Dinner Experience
blurb: Multi-course dinner paired with Caymus wines — a sit-down food-and-wine event minutes away.

title: Tori Amos: In Times of Dragons Tour
blurb: Tori Amos on tour. Note: listing venue looked off — confirm exact location before buying.

title: Pool Party at Enders Pool
blurb: Baldwin Park resident pool party at Enders — about as close to home as it gets.

title: Sanford Food Truck Fiesta: Summer Nights
blurb: Food trucks, drinks and live music take over 2nd Street in historic downtown Sanford.

title: Frozen: The Musical
blurb: Disney's Frozen as a full Broadway musical at the Dr. Phillips Center.

title: Lionel Richie with Earth, Wind & Fire
blurb: Two legends co-headlining downtown's Kia Center.

title: International Vegan Food Festival
blurb: Plant-based vendors, food and drinks in downtown Orlando.

title: Candlelight: Tribute to The Beatles
blurb: String-quartet Beatles tribute by candlelight inside the Orlando Museum of Art — minutes from Baldwin Park.

title: WNBA All-Star VIP Watch Party
blurb: WLW WNBA All-Star watch party with food, drinks and DJ.

title: Kaliente Fest 2026
blurb: Latin music festival with live acts and food vendors.

title: Vine & Dine: Global Bites & Great Wine
blurb: Global small plates, wine and live music at historic Dubsdread — a short drive from Baldwin Park.

title: Orlando Farmers Market at Lake Eola
blurb: Seasonal produce, local art, music, food trucks, beer garden by the lake.

title: Winter Park Saturday Farmers' Market
blurb: Produce, baked goods, plants, local makers a few minutes from Baldwin Park.

title: EPCOT International Food & Wine Festival
blurb: World Showcase global tasting marketplaces + Eat to the Beat concerts. Nearly 3 months.

title: Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party
blurb: Trick-or-treating, exclusive parade & fireworks, character greetings in costume.

title: Animate! Orlando
blurb: Anime & pop-culture convention at the OCCC.

title: Halloween Horror Nights 35
blurb: 10 all-new haunted houses, scare zones, live shows. Earliest start ever.

title: Jack LeFlore Golf Tournament
blurb: Charity scramble at Eagle Creek — soonest confirmed local golf event.

title: Bubbles, Bourbon & Birdies
blurb: Charity golf with bubbles and bourbon at Shingle Creek.

title: Aerospace & Aviation Day
blurb: Aircraft displays and aviation showcase in Sanford — closest local stand-in for an air show.

title: Run Club for MFs that Don't Run
when: Weekly (Tue) · location changes each week — announced on IG. Next: Tue 6/30 @ Andretti Orlando
blurb: Orlando's light-hearted, short-route social run club — 17.4K followers. Pub/venue changes weekly, so the only way to track it is Instagram.

title: Orlando Runners Club (ORC)
when: Wed 7PM social 3mi · Fri 6AM Einstein's College Park · Sun 7AM long run · Mon Mellow Mushroom · 3rd Thu Brewlando Maitland
blurb: Orlando's OG running crew since 1976. Several runs a week for all levels, plus pizza & beer socials.

title: Ten10 Run Club
when: Tuesday nights · 1–5 mile options · happy-hour pricing all evening
blurb: Tuesday-night brewery run with multiple course lengths and happy-hour pricing afterward.

title: Ivanhoe Park Brewing Run Club
blurb: Wednesday-night group geared to getting you race-ready for a 5K, beer at the finish.

title: Lake Eola Run & Walk Club
blurb: Thursday group run/walk around Lake Eola starting at Earthy Picks, with a social hangout after.

title: Grit Running Orlando (GRO)
blurb: Community-focused Saturday-morning group run starting at the Williams Family YMCA.

title: Tactical Brewing Running Club
blurb: Brewery run club out of Tactical Brewing — updates posted on Instagram.

title: Home State Run Club
blurb: Brisk 45-minute Monday-night Winter Park run that scores you 15% off your entire tab.

title: Park Brewing Run Club
blurb: Free Monday-night 5K walk/run led by the pros at Core Values Physical Therapy.

title: Beginner Babes Skate (Rollerina Skate Co)
blurb: Beginner-friendly 2–5 mile skate/roll route launching right in Baldwin Park. Walkable from home.

title: Audubon Park Community Market
location: 1842 E Winter Park Rd (Stardust Video & Coffee)
blurb: Year-round Monday-evening market of growers, chefs, artists and musicians in front of Stardust — minutes from Baldwin Park.

title: SWTHZ — Sauna + Cold Plunge
blurb: Private suites with infrared sauna, cold plunge and vitamin-C shower. Closest contrast-therapy spot to Baldwin Park.

title: Perspire Sauna Studio — Winter Park
blurb: Private infrared sauna sessions for recovery and detox, just up the road in Winter Park.

title: Pause Studio — Contrast Therapy (SoDo)
blurb: Structured sauna + cold plunge contrast therapy in private recovery suites in SoDo.

title: UCF Football vs. Bethune-Cookman
blurb: Season opener at the Bounce House.

title: UCF Football vs. Georgia State
blurb: Family Weekend home game.

title: UCF Football vs. TCU (Big 12 Opener)
blurb: Big 12 conference opener vs. TCU.

title: UCF Football vs. BYU (Homecoming)
blurb: Homecoming game vs. BYU.

title: Florida Roundnet — Orlando Pickup Spikeball
blurb: Orlando hub of Florida Roundnet — weekly free pickup Spikeball, all levels. Connect via their Discord/IG.

title: Adult Hockey League — RDV Sportsplex Ice Den
blurb: Beer-league-style adult hockey with divisions for every skill level, closest rink to Baldwin Park.

title: 1st Thursdays — Orlando Museum of Art
location: Orlando Museum of Art, Loch Haven
blurb: Monthly themed art party — local artists, live music, food and drinks. A short hop from Baldwin Park.

title: Access for All (Free Day) — Orlando Museum of Art
location: Orlando Museum of Art, Loch Haven
blurb: Monthly free-admission day to explore the full collection and exhibitions.

title: Rollins Museum of Art (free) + Lectures & Tours
when: Free & open to public · rotating lectures, tours, concerts
location: Rollins Museum of Art, 1000 Holt Ave
blurb: Free campus art museum with visiting-scholar lectures, docent tours and free public concerts (Annie Russell Theatre nearby).

title: AIA Orlando — Architecture Talks & Site Tours
when: Rotating events — lectures, jobsite tours, panels
blurb: 900-member AIA chapter hosting architect lectures, behind-the-scenes construction tours (OCAD) and student panels.

title: 1 Million Cups Orlando (Guppy Tank)
when: Every Wednesday · 8:30 AM meet-and-greet, 9-10 AM pitches
location: Tech Hub Orlando, 36 W. Pine St
blurb: Two local founders pitch a raw idea in 6 minutes, then the room feedback-storms them for 20. Nearly 800 founders have presented. Free coffee and donuts.

title: Quick Pitch Night (Orlando Innovation League)
when: Periodic evenings · 6:30-8:30 PM · verify next date
location: Tech Hub Orlando, 36 W. Pine St
blurb: Two real Orlando startups face a panel of investor-judges, then a rapid-fire round where anyone can pitch anything in 60 seconds.

title: FamiLAB Hackerspace / Makerspace
when: Open hours + member nights · verify schedule
blurb: Orlando's veteran community hackerspace: laser cutters, 3D printers, electronics benches, woodshop, and a tight maker tribe.

title: MakerFX Makerspace (open house Fridays)
when: Open house every Friday · verify time
blurb: South Orlando's nonprofit makerspace run by the folks behind Maker Faire Orlando. Arduino workshops, laser cutting, 3D printing. Drop in Friday to tour.

title: Orlando Robotics & Makers Club
blurb: Robotics, 3D printing, microcontrollers, RC airplanes, and oddball maker projects all in one club.

title: 3D Print Orlando Community
blurb: A monthly huddle of 3D-printing nerds for show-and-tell, troubleshooting failed prints, and classes for all levels.

title: Orlando Amateur Radio Club (W4PLB)
when: Monthly meeting ~7:30 PM; license test 1st Wed 5:30 PM
location: Beardall Senior Center, 800 S. Delaney Ave
blurb: Central Florida's oldest, largest ham radio club (300+ members) with technical talks. They also throw HamCation, a huge hamfest.

title: Orlando Board Gaming Weekly Meetup
when: Every Thursday and Saturday night · verify time
blurb: A big weekly board-game night (30-80 people) with a deep shared library. Show up solo and get pulled into a game within minutes.

title: O'Town Toastmasters Club
blurb: A lunchtime public-speaking workshop in the Milk District where you practice impromptu and prepared talks in a low-stakes room.

title: Orlando Python User Group
blurb: Twice-monthly gathering of Python users, students and pros for talks and project show-and-tell. Bring a snippet you're stuck on.

title: Orlando Camera Club
when: Monthly meetings + field trips (2nd/3rd Tuesdays) · verify
blurb: Workshops, photo walks, monthly competitions and special-interest groups for people serious about the craft.

title: SCCA Autocross (Central Florida Region)
when: Monthly Solo events; next Orlando dates Aug 2 & Nov 8, 2026 (verify)
blurb: Run your own car through a cone course against the clock with the SCCA's grassroots Solo program. Thrash a parking lot legally.

title: Orlando Kart Center Endurance & League
when: 2-hr enduros twice monthly; Wed money race; 20-race league
location: Orlando Kart Center, 9290 Florida Rolling Hills Rd
blurb: Orlando's only outdoor track: a near-mile course with 45+ mph karts, two-hour team endurance races and a real points championship.

title: OSW Drift Nights
when: Roughly monthly; beginner & advanced run groups (verify dates)
location: Orlando Speed World, 19164 E Colonial Dr
blurb: Central Florida's premier drift track runs two tracks at once for max seat time, with chill beginner days and Pro-Am battles.

title: Orlando Club Sport Social Leagues
when: Seasonal leagues year-round: kickball, dodgeball, cornhole, volleyball
blurb: The big social-sports machine in town: sign up solo or with friends, all built around the post-game bar hang. Adult recess.

title: OUT Sports League (LGBTQ+)
when: Year-round seasons: kickball, dodgeball, volleyball, soccer, cornhole
blurb: Central Florida's premier LGBTQ+ rec league mixes kickball and dodgeball with a packed calendar of free social events.

title: Aiguille Rock Climbing Center
when: Open daily; drop-in climbing, leagues & meetups (verify league nights)
blurb: A 10,000 sq ft Central Florida climbing institution since 1997 with top-rope, bouldering and training walls.

title: Skydive DeLand
when: Open every day; tandems, AFF, sport jumping
blurb: Florida's legendary dropzone (since 1982) where tandem skydiving was invented. An hour up I-4 for a 120 mph freefall.

title: Orlando Dragon Boat Club
when: Regular practices/paddles at Lake Fairview (verify schedule)
blurb: Twenty paddlers, one 40-foot dragon-headed boat, and a charity mission. A genuinely weird team-powered water sport.

title: Orlando Roller Derby
blurb: A real WFTDA flat-track league with travel and home teams. Catch a bout or, if you skate, try out for the Ozone Slayers.

title: ORL BMX Race Nights
when: Thursday races 7:30 PM; Saturday races 8:00 PM
location: Barnett Park BMX, 4801 W Colonial Dr
blurb: Volunteer-run USA BMX track with real gate-drop racing for all ages twice a week. Register and send it off the start hill.

title: MultiGP FPV Drone Racing (Central FL)
when: Local chapter races & practice sessions (verify chapter)
blurb: MultiGP literally started in Central Florida in 2015. Strap on goggles and rip 100 mph FPV quads through gates.

title: Tenoroc Sporting Clays & 5-Stand
when: Open shooting days; sporting clays, 5-stand, trap, archery (verify hours)
blurb: State-run shooting preserve ~35 min away with a 15-station sporting clays arc, 5-stand, trap and an elevated archery range.

title: Sword Masters Club Fencing
when: Ongoing classes & open bouting in epee, foil, saber (verify schedule)
blurb: Orlando's Olympic fencing academy with recreational and competitive tracks in all three weapons.

title: The Villages Polo Club Matches
when: Spring season Mar-mid-May: Fri 3 PM & Sun 1 PM; fall Oct-Dec
blurb: Real outdoor polo an hour from Orlando: tailgate the sidelines and watch matches thunder by. Old-money afternoon for twenty bucks.

title: Defy Sports Park Paintball & Airsoft
when: Walk-on games day & night; check site for open-play days
blurb: Large outdoor fields built for team-based paintball and airsoft, day or night. Walk on solo and get folded into a squad.

title: Orange Blossom Beekeepers Association
when: 3rd Thursday monthly 7 PM; hands-on workshop following Saturday 9 AM
location: UF/IFAS Orange County Extension, 6021 S Conway Rd
blurb: A real-deal honeybee club where backyard beekeepers swap hive gossip; no membership needed to show up. Workshops put frames in your hands.

title: Orange Audubon Society Field Trips
when: Weekly Mead Garden walks, monthly Leu Gardens trips, Sep-Jul
blurb: Florida's second-largest Audubon chapter runs beginner bird walks, young-birder workshops and a December birding festival.

title: Central Florida Genealogical Society
when: Evening 3rd Tue 7:30 PM (Sep-May); daytime 3rd Mon 1-3 PM
blurb: Over 80 meetings a year and five special-interest groups for the family-tree obsessed. Records deep-dives and ancestor detective work.

title: Central Florida Railroad Modelers Open Layout
when: Saturdays 10 AM-4 PM; 3rd Sat operating session; 3 public open houses/yr
location: Clubhouse near Silver Star Rd & N John Young Pkwy
blurb: 1,500 feet of HO-scale dual mainline recreating 1945-1965 West Virginia with custom CTC signaling. Miniature-railroad heaven.

title: Regional Orlando Applied Rocketry (ROAR) Launch
location: 1300 N Fort Christmas Rd, Christmas, FL
blurb: NAR chapter #795 fires off model rockets in a cow pasture east of Orlando. Bring your own bird or watch the engines roar.

title: Kirk Sullens Blacksmithing Classes
when: Flexible schedule, max 2-3 students; verify availability
blurb: An hour from Orlando, a tiny forge where you hammer hot iron and basic knives one-on-one. Closest real anvil-and-coal blacksmithing.

title: Urban Sketchers Orlando
when: Monthly on-location sketch outings; verify each month
blurb: A 1,000+ member chapter of the global on-location drawing movement. Pick a spot, plant a stool, draw the city from life.

title: Orlando Poetry Slam @ Milk Bar
location: The Milk Bar Lounge, 2424 E Robinson St
blurb: The official Orlando slam — three minutes ten seconds to bring the house down. Free to watch, brutal to compete.

title: SAK Comedy Lab Improv
when: Tue-Sat nights; cheap-seat Tue 9 PM ($2), Wed 9 PM ($3)
location: SAK Comedy Lab, 55 W Church St, Ste 211
blurb: Orlando's legendary improv institution — Duel of Fools and dirt-cheap weeknight shows to test the rookies.

title: Enzian Cult Classics Series
when: Periodic screenings, often in 35mm; check calendar
location: Enzian Theater, 1300 S Orlando Ave, Maitland
blurb: A single-screen cinema cafe screening cult favorites in real 35mm with a beer and a burger.

title: Cool Stuff Games — Friday Night Magic
when: Friday Night Magic + board game nights weekly; verify store schedule
location: Multiple stores incl. Maitland & Waterford Lakes
blurb: TCG tournaments, Friday Night Magic, and board-game nights at one of Central Florida's biggest game-store chains.

title: GEEK'd Warhammer Battles & Painting
when: Painting workshops, tabletop battles & campaign events; verify schedule
blurb: Warhammer 40K and Age of Sigmar HQ with painting workshops, tabletop skirmishes and campaign nights for the grimdark faithful.

title: OLO Comic & Toy Show
when: Periodic shows at the Fairgrounds; verify next date
location: Central Florida Fairgrounds, 4603 W Colonial Dr
blurb: Tables of vintage toys, comics and oddball collectibles. A swap-meet for the action-figure-and-longbox crowd.

title: Florida Cabin Fever Quilters Guild
when: 1st Tuesday monthly; social 5 PM, program 6 PM
location: Marks Street Senior Rec Complex, 99 E Marks St
blurb: Downtown's quilting circle with block-of-the-month challenges, sit-and-sews and an annual charity raffle quilt.

title: Orlando Death Collective: Death Cafe
when: Every other month ~7-9 PM; verify 2026 dates
location: OC Library, South Trail Branch, 4600 S Orange Blossom Trl
blurb: Judgment-free gathering to talk frankly about death and dying over coffee. Equal parts morbid and weirdly life-affirming.

title: Silent Book Club Downtown Orlando
when: Monthly; 30 min chat, 1 hr silent reading, then mingle (verify date)
location: Downtown Orlando (venue varies; RSVP via IG)
blurb: Bring your own book and read in companionable silence with strangers. No assigned reading or homework.

title: Knights Under the Stars — Robinson Observatory
when: Each semester; evening star parties, register 1 week prior (verify)
blurb: Free public star party with a 20-inch telescope plus volunteer scopes pointed at planets and deep-sky objects.

title: Seminole State Planetarium Star Party
when: Periodic free outdoor viewing nights (verify dates)
location: Emil Buehler Planetarium, 100 Weldon Blvd, Sanford
blurb: Free telescope viewing of the moon, Jupiter and Saturn at the south pavilion, run with the Central Florida Astronomical Society.

title: Orlando Contra Dance
when: 1st & 3rd Fridays, usually with live music (verify each date)
cost: Free for students/beginners; small fee otherwise
blurb: Florida's only gender-neutral contra series. Live old-time band, a caller, and no partner or experience needed.

title: 4Girvins Farm-to-Table Dinner
when: Ticketed dinner series; farm tour + multi-course meal (verify dates)
blurb: Farm tour with the owners then a chef-curated multi-course meal built around that week's harvest. By The Dinner Party Project.

title: Orlando Supper Club (secret pop-up dinners)
when: Roving pop-up dinners; location often revealed day-of (verify)
blurb: Underground-style supper club hosting dinners all over town, with the spot sometimes kept secret until the day.

title: The Courtesy — Cocktail Class
location: The Courtesy Bar, 1288 N Orange Ave
blurb: Hands-on mixology class at a craft cocktail bar, building seasonal drinks that change every few months.

title: Greenwood Cemetery Moonlight Walking Tour
when: Monthly near the full moon (verify availability)
blurb: Sexton-led two-hour tour of Orlando's 1888 pioneer cemetery by moonlight, focused on real history rather than cheap ghost gags.

title: American Ghost Adventures — Paranormal Investigations
when: Year-round 2-hour tours with real ghost-hunting gear (verify nights)
location: Downtown Orlando, Mount Dora, Sanford, DeLand
blurb: Longest-running local ghost tour: haunted-building history plus hands-on paranormal investigation with actual equipment.

title: Orange Audubon Bird Walks & Field Trips
when: Monthly field trips (~8am); sign-ups open 1-2 mo prior
location: Orlando Wetlands (Christmas), Mead Garden, Lake Apopka
area: Winter Park / Christmas (near home for Mead)
blurb: Local Audubon birders lead early-morning walks where the dawn chorus and nesting spoonbills steal the show. Mead Garden is minutes away.

title: Audubon Center for Birds of Prey
blurb: Eye-to-eye with 50+ non-releasable raptors, bald eagles, owls, kites, falcons, at this rehab center 15 minutes from home.

title: Central Florida Slackline Meetups
when: A few times a month at Orlando parks; check FB, verify
blurb: A loose crew that strings lines between trees and trades balance tips a few times a month. Shoes off and try walking the webbing.

title: Wild Spring Swimming at Wekiwa & Rock Springs
location: Wekiwa Springs SP / Kelly Park Rock Springs / Alexander Springs
blurb: Crystal-clear 68-72F spring water year-round, swim the basin at Wekiwa or float the natural tube run at Rock Springs.

title: Cavern Diving Intro (Dayo Scuba)
when: Scheduled courses; classroom in Orlando, dives at the springs. Verify
blurb: First taste of the overhead environment with an Orlando-based shop, classroom local, though the actual cave dives (Ginnie, etc.) are 2+ hours northwest.

title: Mounted Archery Clinic (Umatilla)
blurb: Loose an arrow from a galloping horse, world-class bowyer Lukas Novotny runs intro clinics on his Umatilla farm. No horse needed.

title: Sunrise Hot-Air Balloon Flight
when: Daily at sunrise, weather permitting (book ahead)
location: Bob's Balloons / Orlando Balloon Rides, Davenport
blurb: Lift off an hour before dawn and drift silently over Central Florida's lakes and orange groves.

title: Central Florida Kite Club & Beach Kite Festivals
when: Club fly-ins + beach festivals; rokkaku battles. Verify next date
location: New Smyrna Beach, Cocoa Beach, Titusville
blurb: Big-sky beach kite gatherings with giant show kites and rokkaku fighting battles. Newcomers welcome, just show up and fly.

title: Stargazing at Crosby Observatory
location: Crosby Observatory, Orlando Science Center, Loch Haven
blurb: Peer through Florida's largest public refractor at Saturn's rings and Jupiter's moons, minutes from home atop the Science Center.

title: Mount Dora Plein Air Paint Out
blurb: Artists fan out across lakeside Mount Dora to paint en plein air, with public awards and a People's Choice vote.
source: Mount Dora Center for the Arts

title: Orlando Underwater Hockey (Octopush)
location: Rosen YMCA Aquatic Center, 8422 International Dr
blurb: A genuinely active, multi-state-champion octopush club (founded 2011) that welcomes total beginners. If you can swim to the bottom, they'll teach you.

title: Central Florida Dog & Disc Club
when: Regular jams & Skyhoundz/UpDog comps; verify on FB
blurb: A real club of disc-dog enthusiasts who meet to throw freestyle and toss-and-fetch with their dogs and run sanctioned qualifiers.

title: Oh La La Pétanque Club — Sylvan Lake Park
when: Weekly play; monthly tournaments (verify on FB)
location: Sylvan Lake Park, 845 Lake Markham Rd, Sanford
blurb: An 18-court pétanque setup with an active club running weekly pickup boules and monthly tournaments. One of the largest in Central Florida.

title: Altamonte Springs Bocce League
blurb: A city-run organized bocce league with a full fall-to-spring season, a structured alternative to brewery pickup courts.

title: Florida BBQ Association Contests Calendar
blurb: The FBA's official competition page is the live source for sanctioned cook-offs, the place to find the next Central Florida contest to spectate or enter.

title: Central Florida Dog Sports — dock diving, barn hunt
when: NADD splashes & barn hunt trials throughout the year
blurb: A dedicated sport-dog facility hosting NADD dock diving and barn hunt trials, part of a real Orlando-area dog-sports circuit.

title: USA Memory Championship — Full Sail University
when: Finals late August (annual); online qualifiers earlier
blurb: The national memory championship finals, names-and-faces, speed cards, speed numbers, are held right in Winter Park. Spectators welcome.

title: BSides Orlando + Lockpick Village
blurb: A community security conference with a Lockpick Village, soldering and social-engineering villages, the local hub for the locksport/TOOOL crowd to pick locks hands-on.

title: Orlando Ultimate — Blue Jacket Park pickup & league
when: Sundays 8:30/10 AM at Blue Jacket Park; pickup most days; Spring/Fall leagues
blurb: Seasonal leagues plus near-daily pickup, including Sunday games and Goaltimate right at Blue Jacket Park, literally in your neighborhood.

title: Central Florida Bonsai Club
blurb: Active 50+ year club with monthly tree-styling demos, workshops and an annual Leu Gardens exhibit. A gateway into suiseki viewing-stones too.

title: Florida's Carnivorous Plant Society
blurb: Florida's Venus-flytrap and pitcher-plant crowd, tied to Kenny Coogan's talks and Bok Tower carnivorous-plant sales. Bog-builders welcome.

title: Peace River Guided Shark-Tooth & Fossil Dig
area: SW of Orlando (~90 min day trip)
blurb: Canoe out with a guide to sift the Peace River for Miocene shark teeth, megalodon, ray plates and Ice Age mammal bone. You keep what you find.

title: Ace Cafe Orlando Thursday Bike Night
location: Ace Cafe Orlando, 100 W Livingston St
blurb: Weekly motorcycle night at the London-born motor-diner downtown, with the Rockers Speed Shop and a cafe-racer scene on site. Hub for vintage-bike wrenchers.

title: Cafe-Moto — Florida Cafe Racer & Vintage Moto Club
blurb: A Florida cafe-racer and vintage-motorcycle club that has partnered with Ace Cafe and Dime City Cycles for cafe-racer shows. The crowd for old-bike restoration.

title: Orlando Audio Meetup (field recording / foley)
blurb: Monthly gathering of audio pros and hobbyists into recording, sound design and field/foley work, heavily fed by the Full Sail community.

title: New Smyrna Beach Sand Art Festival
when: Annual on the beach; verify date
cost: Free to watch; entry to compete
blurb: Competitors carve sculptures in 10x10 beach plots with adult, child and team categories. The closest hands-on competitive sand-sculpture scene.
source: New Smyrna Beach Sand Art Festival

title: Guided Sound Bath at Timucua
location: Timucua Arts Foundation, 2000 S Summerlin Ave
blurb: Crystal-singing-bowl sound immersion with breathwork in Timucua's acoustically gorgeous concert house. Bring a mat, pillow and blanket.

title: Gong Fu Cha Tea Ceremony Workshop
when: Periodic 2-hour hands-on workshops; check for next date
location: East End Market, 3201 Corrine Dr (Chrysalis Tea Co)
blurb: Tea specialist Molly Ward guides a hands-on gong fu cha session, tasting three teas with traditional teaware at Audubon Park's East End Market.

title: Greenwood Cemetery Paranormal Investigation
blurb: A hands-on EVP/ghost-hunt of Orlando's most historic cemetery, learning to use real investigation gear over two hours.

title: Ethos Bushcraft & Survival Course
when: Weekend courses through the year; verify dates
location: FSW Deep Woods Training Facility, Paisley
area: Lake County / Ocala forest edge (~55 min)
blurb: Hands-on primitive skills, fire by friction, cordage and knots, shelter, navigation, water filtration, in deep woods north of Orlando. All levels.

title: Letterboxing Stamp-Hunt (Atlas Quest)
location: Parks across Orlando, Winter Park, Maitland, Apopka
blurb: Analog treasure hunt, carve a personal stamp, follow clues to weatherproof boxes hidden in local parks, swap imprints in the logbooks.

title: Ecstatic Dance Orlando
when: Roughly monthly evenings; follow for dates
blurb: Freeform barefoot dance with live DJ sets, no talking and no phones on the floor, a consent-based, all-bodies-welcome movement container.

title: Goblin Market — Discount Art Mart
when: Roughly monthly, 1-7 PM (themed each time — check site for next date)
blurb: Orlando's discount/deadstock/misprint art & vintage market — trading, cosplay, crafting, food trucks, live music and performances. Nonprofit-run, free to attend.

title: Orlando Oddities and Curiosities Market
location: Event Center at Osceola Heritage Park, 1901 Chief Osceola Trail, Kissimmee
blurb: Florida's largest oddities event — 200+ vendors of taxidermy, morbid antiques, wet specimens and curiosity-shop art, plus sideshow performances (Dead City Circus, The Lizard Man) and taxidermy classes.

title: Old Town Kissimmee Saturday Night Classic Car Cruise
when: Every Saturday, register 1:00-7:30 PM, cruise 7-11 PM
location: Old Town Entertainment District, 5770 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee
blurb: Nearly 35-year tradition — up to 250 pre-1985 hot rods, classics and antiques registered on-site, cruising Old Town's brick streets to live music. Monthly $1,000 cash giveaway for entrants.
